text: They don't teach this in driving school.
Become a perfect driver with these tips.
You can park perfectly anywhere. Just
follow these simple steps. Move close to
the front of the car on your right until
your right mirror is aligned with it.
Turn the steering wheel one and a half
times to the left and slowly move
forward until you see the parking line
touch your car's door handle in the
mirror. Then turn the wheel one and a
half times to the right and start
reversing.
Straighten the wheel and continue
backing up until your car fits perfectly
into the parking
space. Parallel parking. Approach the
car in front and stop when your mirror
aligns with the side mirror of the car
next to you. This is the perfect
starting
position. Turn the wheel one and a half
times to the left and slowly reverse
while watching your right mirror. When
your rear door handle aligns with the
car behind,
stop. Straighten the wheel and keep
reversing until your rear right tire
touches the parking spaces center line.
Now turn the wheel one and a half times
to the right and continue reversing
until your car is perfectly parallel to
the curb. Many drivers make the same
mistake. They turn the wheel immediately
when pulling out. Here's the correct way
to do it. First, move forward without
turning the wheel. Keep going until the
end of your driver's door aligns with
the front of the car next to you. Only
then should you turn the wheel in the
desired direction and continue driving
out. This way, you'll avoid hitting the
nearby vehicle. These simple methods
will protect your car from unnecessary
damage and make parking in tight spots
much easier.
